  "findings": [
    "Spring can travel pins (hydrostatic test stops) were not pulled/removed after hydrostatic testing was completed."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Verify hydrostatic testing is fully complete and signed off.",
    "Carefully remove the travel pins/stops from all spring hangers to allow them to become active.",
    "Verify cold settings match the engineered drawings after pin removal."
  ],
  "rationale": "Spring hanger travel pins (or hydro stops) lock the spring in a rigid position to support the extra weight of water during hydrostatic testing. Failing to remove these pins after the hydro-test prevents the spring from absorbing thermal expansion and contraction during operation, which can transfer massive, unintended loads to the piping system and equipment nozzles, potentially causing catastrophic failure.",
  "references": [
    "MSS SP-58",
    "Project Support Standards"
  ]
}
